treatments.name: Swedish Relaxation Massage
treatments.description: A full-body relaxation massage using long, flowing strokes to ease tension and improve circulation.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Deep Tissue Massage
treatments.description: Targets deep muscle layers to release chronic tension. Ideal for sports recovery and back pain.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Couples Retreat Package
treatments.description: Side-by-side massages, floral bath ritual, herbal tea, and fresh fruit platter — the perfect anniversary experience.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 6 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Ayurvedic Head Ritual
treatments.description: Traditional Shirodhara and scalp massage using warm herbal oils. Deeply calming and restorative.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Ella Glow Facial
treatments.description: A hydrating facial using local botanical extracts — green tea, coconut milk, and turmeric.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 2 hours in advance.

classes.name: Sunrise Yoga
classes.description: Open-air yoga session overlooking the Ella hills. All levels welcome.

classes.name: Mindfulness Meditation
classes.description: Guided meditation in our garden pavilion. A perfect way to begin your day.

classes.name: Aqua Aerobics
classes.description: Fun and energetic pool workout suitable for all fitness levels.

packages.name: Rejuvenation Day Package
packages.description: Full-day wellness immersion: Swedish massage, Ella Glow Facial, Sunrise Yoga, and healthy lunch.

options.name: Airport Arrival Transfer
options.description: Chauffeur-driven transfer from BIA (Colombo) to Ella.

options.name: Airport Departure Transfer
options.description: Chauffeur-driven transfer from Ella to BIA (Colombo).

options.name: Hotel Shuttle (Town)
options.description: Complimentary shuttle to/from Ella town. Departs every 2 hours.

experiences.name: Little Adam's Peak Trek
experiences.description: Guided sunrise trek to the iconic Little Adam's Peak. Witness breathtaking views of Ella Gap and the Nine Arches Bridge. Suitable for most fitness levels with gentle gradients.

experiences.name: Sri Lankan Cooking Class
experiences.description: Learn to prepare authentic Sri Lankan curries, hoppers, and string hoppers with our head chef. You'll cook — and then eat — a full lunch.

experiences.name: Tea Plantation & Factory Tour
experiences.description: Immerse yourself in Sri Lanka's iconic tea culture. Walk through lush tea gardens, watch the plucking process, and tour a working tea factory.

experiences.name: Nine Arches Bridge Cycling Tour
experiences.description: Cycle through Ella's scenic countryside to the famous Nine Arches Bridge. Timed to coincide with the train crossing for the perfect photo.

experiences.name: Stargazing Evening
experiences.description: Ella's high altitude and clear night skies make it perfect for stargazing. Our guide will introduce you to the Southern Hemisphere constellations with a high-powered telescope.

localPlaces.name: Nine Arches Bridge
localPlaces.description: A breathtaking 91 m-long colonial-era railway viaduct built entirely of brick, stone, and cement — without a gram of steel — by local labourers during British rule. Surrounded by lush tea estates, it is arguably Sri Lanka's most photographed sight. The real magic happens when the blue train slowly rolls across, framed by green hills.
localPlaces.travelTime: 10 min by tuk-tuk or 25–30 min walk
localPlaces.hotelNote: The train crosses at approximately 08:30 and 15:15 daily (timings vary — ask our concierge the evening before for the exact schedule). Arrive 20 minutes early for a good position.

localPlaces.name: Little Adam's Peak
localPlaces.description: The most accessible and rewarding hike in Ella — a well-maintained tea-estate trail leading to a twin-peaked summit at 1,141 m with panoramic views of Ella Gap, Nine Arches Bridge, and the southern plains. The ascent takes 30–45 minutes and is suitable for all fitness levels, passing through emerald tea fields with a resident population of friendly cows.
localPlaces.travelTime: 10 min by tuk-tuk to trailhead, then 45 min hike
localPlaces.hotelNote: Our 05:30 guided trek departs daily from reception and includes walking sticks and a packed breakfast at the summit. Book the evening before.

localPlaces.name: Ella Rock
localPlaces.description: The serious hike of the Ella area — a 10 km round-trip through rubber and tea estates, jungle, and rocky ridgelines to the 1,041 m summit with sweeping 360° views of the Ella Valley. The trail is unmarked in places and typically takes 3–4 hours return, requiring good fitness and sturdy footwear. Entry requires a small fee paid at the warden's hut near the top.
localPlaces.travelTime: 15 min by tuk-tuk to start, then 3–4 hr return hike
localPlaces.openingHours: Sunrise recommended; start no later than 07:00
localPlaces.entryFee: Approx. LKR 930 per person (paid at the top)
localPlaces.hotelNote: A guide is strongly recommended as the trail is not well-marked in the upper section. Our concierge arranges experienced local guides from LKR 2,500. Wear closed-toe shoes and carry water.

localPlaces.name: Ravana Falls
localPlaces.description: One of the widest curtain waterfalls in Sri Lanka, plunging 25 m down a broad rock face fed by seasonal rains. Hindu legend holds that the demon king Ravana hid Princess Sita in a nearby cave after abducting her from India, giving both the waterfall and the adjacent cave their names. The site is particularly dramatic after monsoon rains.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Pair with a visit to Ravana Cave (2 km from Ella town, LKR 250 entry) — 600+ steps lead to a cave and small temple with valley views. Both can be done in a single tuk-tuk trip.

localPlaces.name: Demodara Loop (Spiral Railway)
localPlaces.description: One of the world's most audacious feats of railway engineering — at Demodara station, the track makes a full spiral loop through a tunnel in the hill and emerges below itself, gaining altitude in a space too tight for a conventional gradient. Built by the British in 1921, the loop allows passengers to wave at the same train they were just on, passing on the track below.
localPlaces.travelTime: 20 min by tuk-tuk, or a scenic 10-min train ride from Ella station
localPlaces.openingHours: Viewable at any time; trains pass several times daily
localPlaces.entryFee: Train ticket Ella–Demodara from LKR 30 (third class)
localPlaces.hotelNote: Taking the short train ride from Ella station to Demodara and back (30 min round trip) is highly recommended over just viewing the loop. Book tickets at Ella station — third class is an experience in itself.

localPlaces.name: Lipton's Seat Viewpoint
localPlaces.description: At 1,970 m above sea level atop the Dambatenne Tea Estate, this panoramic viewpoint was a favourite of Sir Thomas Lipton, the Scottish tea magnate, who would survey his vast estate from this spot on horseback. On a clear day, the view extends across hundreds of kilometres of rolling tea country — on misty mornings, the sea of clouds below is equally magical.
localPlaces.openingHours: Accessible daily; best 06:00–09:00 before cloud sets in
localPlaces.entryFee: LKR 100 (estate entry charge at the gate)
localPlaces.hotelNote: The drive through the Dambatenne estate is itself scenic. Leave by 05:30 for clear skies — by 10:00 the summit is usually in cloud. Combine with a visit to the Dambatenne Tea Factory at the base of the hill.
